cases.setup: A vendor/package Firmware Compatibility Claim is retained as evidence and separately evaluated against exact live Device/Profile/Interface/Connection/Provider context.
A model/chipset/tool support Claim directly authorizes a physical write.
Vendor and tool Claims disagree for the same package/device family.
A broad source Claim is mutated in place into an exact Compatibility Result.
One ADB/Fastboot/META protocol interaction maps to exact WP02 Activity, Operation and new Attempt while retaining Device/Session/Interface/Connection/Provider context.
Device Protocol Operation ID replaces WP02 Operation or Attempt identity.
A Device Protocol Operation links to one specialist Firmware Operation for a write stage.
Firmware Operation and Device Protocol Operation are represented by one generic record without Plan/Authorization/Verification separation.
assertions: two typed projections required where both scopes apply

cases.setup: Backend acknowledgement moves Device Protocol Operation directly to completed_verified.
Policy permits acknowledgement-only completion under a narrow explicit limitation.
A protocol retry reuses the previous WP02 Attempt or nonce.
Protocol execution uses an old Connection epoch, Provider generation, Lease or fence.
An uncertain physical operation opens lifecycle-bearing Recovery with exact previous epoch/generation, reacquires a new context, reconciles state and concludes safe_to_retry_new_attempt.
A 0.1.0 Recovery record without lifecycle or prior/new generation evidence is relabelled 0.1.1.
assertions: manual review or preserve legacy required

cases.setup: Recovery resumes using the previous Session, Lease, fence or Provider generation after reconnect.
Recovery declares safe_to_retry_new_attempt without proving whether the prior effect occurred.
Evidence remains insufficient to prove physical state.
Recovery overwrites original uncertain Operation/Receipts with a successful state.
Catalog 0.1.2 resolves 47 unique schemas and 15 unique lifecycle machines offline.
Catalog 0.1.1 is treated as final despite missing registered Claim, Protocol Operation and Recovery lifecycle contracts.
A new device.lease entity is introduced alongside runtime.lease.
A second canonical Domain decomposition-run identity replaces object.decomposition_run.
